what_it_does: This file is an interpretive ledger entry for the claude-ds project that summarizes, structures, and evaluates the INTEGRATION.md design-system contract, producing observations, conventions, concerns, themes, and embedding-ready summaries.
observations: Records project 'claude-ds', original path 'analysis/INTEGRATION.md', and model 'opus'.
Frames the target document as the wiring/anti-drift contract for the ConceptV design system.
Lists four single-source registries — Tokens, Types, Engine, AI — with their home files and consumer APIs.
Notes that all four registries are intended to share the same API shape: register/resolve/lineage/query/subscribe.
Maps token layering from L0 primitives through L1 semantic roles and L2 component tokens to consumers.
Documents entry points for adding colors, atoms, axes, and generative-core behavior.
Lists guardrails: check_design_system compiler, adherence lint, specimen cards, and deprecate-don't-delete.
Generalizes the single-source rule to the type graph and AI capability/provider catalog.
Closes with compressed formulas design=f(content,axisPosition) and ai=f(capability,context,params).
Includes evaluative metadata fields: purpose_vs_actual, apparent_themes, intention_signals, novelty, contribution, and embedding summaries.
standouts: Four very different subsystems (CSS tokens, type graph, render engine, AI catalog) are treated as instances of one registry pattern.
Voice is treated as data: voice.conceptv is sourced via CV_AI, never inlined into prompts.
A loud-never-silent rule is applied to missing AI capabilities, providers, and runtimes.
The entire design+AI system is framed as two parallel f(...) functions computed from drift-free registries.
conventions: registry-is-truth / single-source-of-truth: one home per concept, consumers reference rather than copy.
fail-loud: missing entries throw instead of returning degraded defaults.
token-layering: define at the lowest valid layer, reference higher layers with var().
extend-by-registration: add new values/types/axes/capabilities by registering at the right layer.
axis-as-data-knob: new axes use data-* attributes plus CSS variables.
Interpretive-layer schema conventions: observations, standouts, concerns, notes, questions, purpose_vs_actual, themes, novelty, contribution, and embedding summaries.
concerns: The specific count '43 capabilities across 14 families today' is prose that will age silently.
The claim that all four registries share the same API shape is stated as design intent, not verified in this file.
Some evaluative claims (e.g., purpose_vs_actual 'Match') are interpretations not sourced within the file itself.
Embedding summaries encode inferred intention that may not be explicit in the original document.
notes: An interpretive ledger record that distills the INTEGRATION.md anti-drift contract into structured metadata, themes, and embedding-ready summaries.
questions: Does this interpretive layer transcribe the '43 capabilities' count from the original document, or is it an added inference?
How is the shared register/resolve/lineage/query/subscribe API shape verified — by code, by the original doc, or by assumption?
What is the lineage/subscribe mechanism and is it implemented for all four registries?
purpose_vs_actual: Match — the file states it is an interpretive analysis of INTEGRATION.md and delivers structured observations, conventions, concerns, themes, and summaries consistent with that role.
intention_signals: The file is trying to turn a design-system integration document into a machine-readable interpretive record: extracting the governing contract, surfacing conventions and risks, and producing embedding summaries so the ledger can later query, cluster, and fuse related substrates without re-reading the original prose.
novelty: It is a meta-interpretation: not the design contract itself, but a structured ledger entry about that contract, with explicit fields for purpose-vs-actual, novelty, contribution, and embedding-ready summaries.
contribution: Contributes a structured, queryable interpretation of the INTEGRATION.md contract — including its four-registry mapping, conventions, concerns, themes, and embedding summaries — that a fused system can use to locate and reason about the anti-drift contract without parsing the original markdown.
summary_for_embedding: Interpretive ledger JSON for the claude-ds project's INTEGRATION.md, capturing the four single-source registry anti-drift contract, shared registry API shape, token layering rules, AI capability conventions, and embedding-ready summaries.
intention_for_embedding: Intended to provide a neutral, searchable, structured summary of the design-system integration contract so the ledger can relate it to other code/design substrates during fusion and querying.
